commit.message:
v1.3.0 — Generic RowProxy: end-to-end type safety for get()

RowProxy<S> accepts a const-narrowed schema tuple and infers the exact
return type of get() per column. Column names are restricted to known
keys, and each resolves to the correct TS type via ColumnTypeMap.

- types.ts: add ColumnTypeMap (ColumnDataType → TS type)
- virtual-chamber.tsx: generic RowProxy<S>, VirtualChamberProps<S>,
  VirtualChamber<S>; internal UntypedRowProxy for implementation
- core/index.ts: export ColumnTypeMap
- Backward compatible: unparameterized RowProxy returns the full union

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: pull --rebase before push to prevent multi-instance divergence

When multiple autobackup instances push to the same repo, they can diverge
if one pushes between another's last pull and current push. Added git pull
--rebase before push to reconcile. Also switched from shell-based git push
to exec.Command with env for safety, and changed Dockerfile git config from
--global to --system so it works with non-root container users.

commit.message:
fix: 将本地终端设置为原始模式以支持实时输入

问题：
- AI CLI 工具（如 claude）的输入不响应
- 只有按回车键后输入才发送到程序
- 原因：终端默认使用行缓冲模式

解决方案：
- 使用 golang.org/x/term 将本地终端设置为原始模式
- 原始模式下每个按键立即发送，无需等待回车键
- 这是交互式 CLI 工具的标准要求
- 程序退出时自动恢复终端状态

现在 AI CLI 工具可以正常响应用户输入了

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Initial release: Mcaster1AudioPipe v1.0.0

Broadcast-grade virtual audio routing application and CoreAudio HAL driver
for macOS, purpose-built for the Mcaster1 ecosystem (AMP → AudioPipe →
DSPEncoder → streaming target).

Core features:
- CoreAudio HAL AudioServerPlugin — userspace, no reboot, SIP-safe
  - Up to 16 simultaneous named virtual pipe devices
  - Lock-free SPSC ring buffers, ARM64 cache-line aligned (128B)
  - Float32 native, 44.1/48/96kHz, mono/stereo/5.1/7.1
  - CFMessagePort IPC server with full input validation
- Qt6 Widgets GUI with enterprise Fusion/QSS styling
  - Pipe list with inline peak+RMS+clip level meters
  - Interactive 3D patch bay (QGraphicsView) — drag-and-drop Bezier cables
  - Driver management tab: HAL plugin list + audio service control
  - Preferences: buffer size, sample rate, notifications
  - 42 custom Retina-ready SVG icons
- PortAudio device enumeration with CoreAudio hot-plug detection
- JSON config persistence with atomic write + symlink/owner/size guards
- SAST-hardened: _Atomic IO fields, NaN/whitelist IPC validation,
  shell-escape, QPointer guard, pipe name regex, compiler hardening flags
- 26/26 offline driver unit tests (no install required)
- macOS DMG packaging via CPack + macdeployqt (self-contained .app)
- GitHub Actions CI: macos-14 (ARM64) + macos-13 (Intel)
- Windows IPC and device enumeration stubs (WDM driver planned)

Phases AP1–AP6 complete. GPL-2.0-or-later.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: Mcaster1AudioPipe
repository.description: Virtual audio device driver that creates system-level audio pipe devices on macOS. Route audio between applications with zero latency — the invisible backbone of the Mcaster1 broadcast ecosystem. Windows and Linux builds coming soon!

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add ROM base address inference from absolute jump/call targets

Analyzes absolute address operands from JMP/JSR/CALL instructions and
raw pointer values to detect when a ROM is loaded at the wrong base
address. Buckets target addresses by ROM-size-aligned ranges and finds
the base where most targets would fall within the ROM.

Tested: correctly identifies NeoGeo BIOS at 0xC00000 and Saturn BIOS
mirror at 0x20000000. No false positives on CPC464, GBA, Genesis ROMs
that are already at the correct base.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: ghidra-heuristic-codefinder

commit.message:
Add starter-nextjs skill: Next.js 16 + Drizzle ORM + PostgreSQL + Clerk + Docker

Complete project bootstrap skill mirroring starter-django but with the Next.js stack:
- Next.js 16 App Router with standalone Docker output
- Drizzle ORM with type-safe schema and pg_advisory_lock migrations
- @clerk/nextjs v7 middleware via proxy.ts (Next.js 16 convention)
- Single-container architecture (no nginx, no separate frontend service)
- Dev uses drizzle-kit push, prod uses SQL migration files
- Multi-stage Dockerfile with serverExternalPackages for migration support

- base_loader.py - base class for all datasets
- mosei_loader.py - CMU-MOSEI dataset
- vqa_loader.py - VQA-v2 dataset
- modality_dropout.py - unified modality dropout

Baselines:
- darts.py - DARTS implementation

All scripts include comprehensive comments and follow TDD principles.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add smm watch for round-trip agent workflow

`smm watch <id>` polls a document and blocks until it changes, then
prints the updated markdown to stdout. Enables the round-trip flow:
agent shares → person edits in browser → agent pulls changes back.

Updated /smm skill with round-trip instructions so Claude Code
knows how to share, wait, and re-import.

Shown next to Return % — hidden when 0 (no snapshot history yet).
Surfaces in state API as cagr_pct.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix PINSYSCALLS stub GC: use __attribute__((used)) instead of -u flags

With LTO (-flto=full), the compiler eliminates unreachable functions before
the linker stage.  The linker's -u/--undefined flag operates on object-file
symbols and cannot prevent LTO from removing functions that are invisible to
the IR optimizer.

System::Call(USIZE) (0-arg, _pir_sc0) and System::Call(...5 args...) (4-arg,
_pir_sc4) were not called by the test binary and were eliminated by LTO, so
their assembly labels resolved to vma=0x0 in .openbsd.syscalls.  pinsyscalls(2)
then registered the wrong PC for those syscall arities → SIGABRT.

Fix: add __attribute__((used)) to all seven System::Call definitions in
system.x86_64.cc.  This attribute is part of the LLVM IR; the LTO optimizer
honours it and always emits the function regardless of reachability.

Remove the now-redundant -u,_pir_sc* linker flags from OpenBSD.cmake.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Add Playwright e2e tests (17 passing across desktop/mobile)

* feat: upgrade Astro 4.9 → 5.17

  @astrojs/tailwind, @astrojs/markdown-remark to latest v5-compatible versions

  (remark-shiki-twoslash → @typescript/vfs triggers it)

* ci: use Node 24

* ci: use Node 24

* fix: add packages field to pnpm-workspace.yaml (pnpm 10)

* ci: let pnpm version come from packageManager field

- Verify twoslash code blocks render on 3 pages
- Check data-lsp type hover info is present
- Verify error annotations on refinement-types
- Add screenshot capture for visual regression

* feat: migrate remark-shiki-twoslash → @shikijs/twoslash

- Remove remark-shiki-twoslash and @typescript/vfs (fixes Node 24 localStorage bug)
- Use Astro 5 built-in Shiki with @shikijs/twoslash transformer
- Update e2e tests for new twoslash selectors
- All 10 twoslash e2e tests passing on Node 24

* chore: remove zaduma-v2-spec.md (moved to workspace)

* fix: use astro:env for OG_IMAGE_SECRET, gracefully degrade when missing

- Migrate OG_IMAGE_SECRET to astro:env schema (typed, server-only, secret)
- Return undefined instead of throwing when secret is not set (fixes CI build)
- Add img.src fallback when img.og is missing (OG image for posts with only src)
- Add PUBLIC_URL to astro:env schema

* revert: restore original ci.yml

Reverts unwarranted CI changes that broke the deploy workflow.
Keeps the original Vercel deploy steps and workflow_dispatch trigger.

* feat: migrate remark-shiki-twoslash → @shikijs/twoslash

- Remove remark-shiki-twoslash and @typescript/vfs (fixes Node 24 localStorage bug)
- Use Astro 5 built-in Shiki with @shikijs/twoslash transformer
- Update CSS for new twoslash selectors (twoslash-hover, twoslash-popup-container)
- Update e2e tests for astro-code class (pre.shiki → pre.astro-code)
- Bump TypeScript 5.4 → 5.8 (peer dep requirement)
- All 17 tests passing on both Node 22 and Node 24

* refactor: remove dead .shiki CSS selectors

All code blocks now use .astro-code / .astro-code-themes classes.
Removed .shiki, data-lsp, data-err, and legacy remark-shiki-twoslash
selectors. ~1500 bytes lighter.

* fix: hide twoslash popups by default, show on hover

Popup containers were rendering permanently visible, obscuring code.
Now hidden with display:none, shown via :hover on .twoslash-hover.

* fix: style twoslash error-line to match prod error block

* ci: trigger on pull_request events

* ci: limit push trigger to main to avoid double runs

* fix: lint error, typecheck error, add pre-push hook

* remove committed githooks (use local .git/hooks instead)

* ci: add Playwright e2e tests, replace lighthouserc with Lighthouse Check action

* ci: add astro sync before typecheck to generate env types

* ci: merge server start + wait into one step

* ci: add pull-requests permission and github token for Lighthouse PR comments

* ci: drop --no-optional (rollup needs native binaries)

* ci: fix test setup — exclude e2e from vitest, add --no-sandbox for puppeteer in CI, run playwright separately

* refactor: consolidate on Playwright, remove vitest+puppeteer

- Port visual regression tests to Playwright toHaveScreenshot()
- Remove puppeteer, pptr-testing-library, jest-image-snapshot, vitest
- Single test runner: playwright test
- CI: one test step instead of two

* fix: cross-platform visual snapshots, add 1% diff tolerance

* fix: make OG_IMAGE_SECRET required, remove PUBLIC_URL from env schema

OG_IMAGE_SECRET must be set or the build fails. No more graceful
degradation — broken admin page is worse than a clear build error.

PUBLIC_URL was redundant — already handled by makePublicURL() via
vite.define.

* ci: fallback OG_IMAGE_SECRET for fork PRs

The --shiki-dark-bg variable (e.g. #24292e from github-dark) was adding
a visible background to code blocks in dark mode. haspar.us prod had

* fix: add twoslash visual regression baselines

- Generate platform-agnostic snapshot baselines (no OS suffix)
- Set snapshotPathTemplate to exclude platform/project from path
- Allow 5% pixel diff ratio for cross-platform font rendering

* fix: swap shiki CSS import order so custom styles override library defaults

* fix: remove @shikijs/twoslash/style-rich.css that overrides custom styles

Library's opacity-based popup show/hide conflicts with our display-based
approach in shiki.css. Also adds unwanted code block backgrounds. Our
shiki.css already covers all twoslash styling.

* chore: remove dead CSS selectors and unused ColorSchemeSelect

Remove ~150 lines of CSS targeting old remark-shiki-twoslash markup
(.code-container, .query, .error/.error-behind, .tag-container,
.logger, .inline-completions) — none matched in built output.

Delete ColorSchemeSelect.tsx — exported but never imported anywhere,
replaced by command palette theme switching.

* Kill the animation

* fix: use CSS anchor positioning for twoslash tooltips

Replaces absolute positioning with CSS Anchor Positioning API so
tooltips escape the pre's overflow clipping and center on the
hovered token. Includes a fallback to flip above when near the
bottom of the viewport.

* fix: add fallback for browsers without CSS anchor positioning

* Fix case direction

* chore: remove zaduma-v2-spec.md (unrelated to migration)

* fix: restore preview deploy condition for push events

* fix: align CI deploy conditions with zaduma

* chore: sync CI workflow with zaduma

- Add PR triggers (pull_request, pull_request_target)
- Add fork-safe deploy conditions and OG_IMAGE_SECRET handling
- Add pull-requests:write permission, merge_commit_sha checkout
- Use pnpm exec for playwright, run vitest + playwright in test step
- Use REF_NAME with head_ref fallback for PR branches
- Use PRODUCTION_HOSTNAME env var

* chore: remove dead vitest/puppeteer infra, add lighthouse CI job

Replace vitest with playwright in test scripts. Remove puppeteer,
jest-image-snapshot and related deps. Add lighthouse + remove-trusted-label
jobs matching zaduma CI.

* fix: use correct GitHub API endpoint to remove trusted label

The label name must be in the URL path, not as a form parameter.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: remove staticDistDir from lighthouserc to fix Lighthouse CI

The collect.staticDistDir config caused the action to start a local
server on a random port and append it to the Vercel deployment URLs,
resulting in NO_FCP errors. The CI workflow already provides explicit
URLs via the urls input.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: gate build-and-deploy on trusted label for pull_request_target

Without this, any label on a fork PR triggers a build with secret
access. Ported from zaduma.

commit.message:
fix: clear token refresh timestamp before gateway restart

When exit code 2 triggers a gateway restart, R2 restore overwrites the
fresh token in config file. Then refresh-gcp-token.sh skips because
the timestamp says "still fresh". Delete /tmp/gcp-token-last-refresh
before restart so the startup refresh actually runs.

Applied to both cron handler and Admin token-refresh endpoint.

repository.name: cligool
repository.description: CLI GoOnline

commit.message:
Full audit: fix 14 issues, add memory system and cleanup

Crash fixes:
- scheduler_cli.py: rewrite to use direct SQLite (was passing wrong args)
- plist template: source .env via bash wrapper (launchd has no EnvironmentFile)
- updater: don't restart mid-response, tell user to /restart

Bot fixes:
- System prompt now includes conversation summary and persistent memories
- Compaction triggers automatically when history exceeds threshold
- Remove double sanitize in media group processing
- Daily reset actually called (was dead code)
- First boot welcome message with machine specs

Core fixes:
- health.py: replace time.sleep(1) CPU check with load average (was blocking event loop)
- skill_loader: instruct LLM to read SKILL.md for full instructions
- wizard: create memory directory structure during install
- service.py: add {{HOME}} template replacement for plist

New features:
- utils/project_manager.py — project state tracking (create/list/status/update)
- utils/cleanup.py — automated maintenance (old attachments, log rotation, temp files)
- /cleanup command registered in bot

README updated with all changes, LLM provider table, session management docs,
memory system docs, and security details.

Co-Authored-By: Claude (claude-opus-4-6) <noreply@anthropic.com>

`get()` + cast
- Annotate why `@final` can't be a native `final` keyword (tests mock
this class)

closes: #11009

repository.name: openemr
repository.description: The most popular open source electronic health records and medical practice management solution.

commit.message:
refactor: drop scalar score from system matrix, reframe as feature breadth

The single capability score collapsed orthogonal taxonomy axes into a
misleading ranking. The checkmark matrix now stands on its own, systems
are sorted alphabetically, and the long-tail analysis uses "feature
breadth count" with an explicit caveat that it measures breadth not
quality. Bar chart replaced with systems-per-category count.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
